activities_projects.title: The Perpendicular Bisector Proof
activities_projects.objective: Prove congruence in triangles formed by a perpendicular bisector
steps: Draw a line segment XY and construct its perpendicular bisector PQ intersecting XY at M
Pick any point Z on the bisector and join it to X and Y
Prove $\triangle ZMX \cong \triangle ZMY$ using the SAS criterion
Conclude why ZX must always equal ZY
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies congruence rules to prove geometric theorems
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical rigor and correct use of symbols (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Diagonal Congruence in Parallelograms
steps: Construct a parallelogram ABCD
Draw the diagonal AC and identify $\triangle ABC$ and $\triangle CDA$
Prove their congruence using the ASA rule (utilizing alternate interior angles)
Identify all pairs of corresponding parts using CPCT
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the relationship between parallel lines and triangle congruence
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of alternate angles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Counter-Example: AAA Fallacy
activities_projects.objective: Deep dive into why AAA does not guarantee congruence
steps: Construct an equilateral triangle with 4cm sides
Construct another equilateral triangle with 8cm sides
Verify that all corresponding angles are equal ($60^{\circ}$)
Write a mathematical argument explaining why these 'twins' fail the congruence test
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically analyzes and refutes invalid congruence criteria
activities_projects.evaluation: Strength of the mathematical argument (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Overlapping Triangles Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Identify and prove congruence in overlapping figures
steps: Draw a large isosceles triangle ABC ($AB=AC$)
Draw two medians, BE and CF, to the equal sides
Identify the overlapping triangles $\triangle BFC$ and $\triangle CEB$
Prove they are congruent using the SAS criterion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops advanced visualization and proof-writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in separating overlapping triangles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: RHS Criterion in Altitudes
activities_projects.objective: Prove congruence of altitudes in an isosceles triangle
steps: Draw $\triangle PQR$ where $PQ=PR$
Construct altitudes PL and QM to the opposite sides
Prove $\triangle QMR \cong \triangle RLQ$ using the RHS rule
State the relationship between the lengths of the altitudes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Mastery of RHS application in height-based problems
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of altitude construction and proof (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Medians of Congruent Triangles
activities_projects.objective: Prove that corresponding medians of congruent triangles are equal
steps: Draw two congruent triangles $\triangle ABC$ and $\triangle DEF$
Construct medians AM and DN to the sides BC and EF respectively
Prove $\triangle ABM \cong \triangle DEN$ using the SAS rule
Justify why $AM = DN$ using CPCT
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that congruence extends to internal line segments
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct construction and logical flow (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Congruence in Circle Geometry
activities_projects.objective: Apply congruence to chords of a circle
steps: Draw a circle with center O and two equal chords AB and CD
Join the endpoints to the center to form $\triangle OAB$ and $\triangle OCD$
Prove these triangles are congruent using the SSS rule
Show that the angles subtended at the center are equal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies triangle congruence to solve circle properties
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision in drawing and radius identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Complex Algebraic CPCT
steps: Given $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle PQR$ with $AB = 2x + 3y$, $PQ = 13$, $BC = 5x - y$, and $QR = 7$
Set up a system of linear equations based on CPCT
Solve for $x$ and $y$
Verify the third side if $AC = 10$ and $PR = 2x + y + 2$
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct algebraic solution and verification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Construction of Indirect Twins
activities_projects.objective: Construct triangles congruent to a given one using only SAS
steps: Draw a random scalene triangle
Using only a compass and straightedge (no protractor), construct a congruent copy
Document each step of the construction
Write a proof explaining why the construction works
activities_projects.evaluation: Success of the construction without measurement tools (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Congruence Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Research and present applications of congruence in architecture
steps: Research how the 'geometric twins' concept is used in truss bridges or the Eiffel Tower
Draw a diagram of a structure showing congruent triangles
Explain why congruence is vital for the stability and balance of the structure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects abstract congruence to structural engineering
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of research and structural analysis (10 marks)
